title: Incorrect WiFi Password
shortDescription: The Ring device cannot connect to WiFi because the entered password is incorrect.
fullDescription:
Error 2 on Ring doorbells and cameras indicates that the WiFi password entered during setup is incorrect. The device attempted to authenticate with the router but was rejected due to a password mismatch.

This is one of the most common Ring setup errors. It frequently occurs when passwords contain special characters that are entered incorrectly, when the password has been recently changed on the router, or when there is confusion between the 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz network passwords.

Most Ring devices only support 2.4 GHz WiFi networks, so ensure you are connecting to the correct band with the correct password.

causes.cause: WiFi password entered incorrectly during setup
WiFi password recently changed on the router
Connecting to 5 GHz network instead of 2.4 GHz
Special characters in password not supported

symptoms: Error 2 displayed in the Ring app during setup
Device fails to connect to WiFi network
Setup process restarts after WiFi connection attempt
Ring device LED flashes indicating connection failure

fixSteps.title: Verify the WiFi password
fixSteps.instruction: Check your WiFi password carefully. Log into your router's admin panel or check the password saved on another device connected to the same network. Pay attention to uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and special characters.

fixSteps.title: Ensure you are using 2.4 GHz
fixSteps.instruction: Most Ring devices only work on 2.4 GHz WiFi. Check your router settings to ensure 2.4 GHz is enabled and note its specific password if it differs from the 5 GHz network.

fixSteps.title: Restart setup with correct password
fixSteps.instruction: In the Ring app, tap Set Up a Device, select your Ring device, and carefully enter the correct 2.4 GHz WiFi password. Use the show password option to verify before submitting.

fixSteps.title: Simplify the WiFi password temporarily
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, temporarily change your WiFi password to something simple without special characters. Complete the Ring setup, then change the password back and update the Ring device.

whenToCallPro: This is always user-fixable. Contact Ring support only if you suspect a hardware WiFi issue after confirming the correct password.
videoSearchQuery: Ring doorbell Error 2 WiFi password fix

faq.q: Does Ring work with 5 GHz WiFi?
faq.a: Most Ring devices only support 2.4 GHz WiFi. Ring Video Doorbell Pro and some newer models support both 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz. Check your model's specifications.

faq.q: How do I find my WiFi password?
faq.a: Check your router's label for the default password, log into your router admin panel (usually 192.168.1.1), or check saved passwords on a device already connected to the network.

title: Unable to Join WiFi Network
shortDescription: The Ring device found the WiFi network but cannot establish a connection.
fullDescription:
Error 3 indicates that the Ring device can see the WiFi network but cannot successfully join it. Unlike Error 2 (wrong password), Error 3 suggests the password was accepted but the connection process failed at a later stage — possibly due to DHCP issues, MAC filtering, network congestion, or interference.

This error is common when the router has MAC address filtering enabled, when the router's DHCP pool is exhausted (too many devices), or when the Ring device is too far from the router for a stable connection.

Router configuration adjustments or moving the Ring device closer to the router usually resolve this issue.

causes.cause: Router MAC address filtering blocking the device
DHCP pool exhausted — too many connected devices
WiFi signal too weak at device location
Router security settings incompatible
Network congestion from too many devices

symptoms: Error 3 displayed in Ring app during setup
Ring device LED spins white then fails
Setup process stalls at 'Joining network' step
Device occasionally connects then immediately disconnects

fixSteps.title: Restart your router
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug your router for 30 seconds, then plug it back in. Wait 2-3 minutes for it to fully restart, then retry the Ring setup.

fixSteps.title: Check MAC address filtering
fixSteps.instruction: Log into your router admin panel and check if MAC filtering is enabled. If so, add the Ring device's MAC address to the allowed list, or temporarily disable MAC filtering during setup.

fixSteps.title: Move the Ring device closer to the router
fixSteps.instruction: During initial setup, bring the Ring device close to the router (within 10 feet). Once connected and configured, you can move it to its intended location.

fixSteps.title: Check DHCP settings
fixSteps.instruction: In your router settings, ensure the DHCP pool has available addresses. The pool should support at least 50 devices. Expand it if necessary.

fixSteps.title: Use WPA2-PSK security
fixSteps.instruction: In your router WiFi settings, set security to WPA2-PSK (AES). Ring devices may have issues with WPA3, WEP, or mixed-mode security settings.

whenToCallPro: If router configuration changes do not resolve the issue, contact Ring support for device-specific troubleshooting or your ISP for router configuration help.
videoSearchQuery: Ring doorbell Error 3 WiFi connection fix

faq.q: What security type should my WiFi use for Ring?
faq.a: Ring devices work best with WPA2-PSK (AES). Avoid WEP (insecure and incompatible) and WPA3 (not supported by all Ring models).

faq.q: How many devices can my router handle?
faq.a: Most home routers support 20-50 simultaneous connections. If you have many smart home devices, consider a mesh WiFi system for better capacity and coverage.

title: DHCP Failure — No IP Address Assigned
shortDescription: The Ring device connected to WiFi but could not obtain an IP address from the router.
fullDescription:
Error 4 means the Ring device successfully connected to the WiFi network but the router's DHCP server did not assign it an IP address. Without an IP address, the device cannot communicate on the network or reach Ring's cloud servers.

This typically happens when the DHCP pool is full (all available IP addresses are assigned to other devices), when the DHCP server on the router is disabled or malfunctioning, or when there is a conflict with another device using the same IP.

Restarting the router refreshes the DHCP leases and usually resolves this issue.

causes.cause: DHCP address pool exhausted
DHCP server disabled on the router
Router DHCP service malfunctioning
IP address conflict with another device

symptoms: Error 4 in the Ring app during setup
Device connects to WiFi but cannot reach the internet
Setup hangs at the connection step
Ring app shows device offline despite WiFi connection

fixSteps.title: Restart the router
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the router power for 30 seconds, plug it back in, and wait 2-3 minutes for full restart. This resets the DHCP server and frees IP addresses.

fixSteps.title: Check DHCP settings
fixSteps.instruction: Log into your router admin panel. Ensure DHCP is enabled and the IP pool range is large enough (e.g., 192.168.1.2 to 192.168.1.254). Expand the range if needed.

fixSteps.title: Remove unused device reservations
fixSteps.instruction: In the router's DHCP reservations or connected devices list, remove any old devices no longer on the network to free up IP addresses.

fixSteps.title: Assign a static IP to the Ring device
fixSteps.instruction: In your router's DHCP settings, create a static IP reservation for the Ring device using its MAC address. This guarantees it always gets an IP.

whenToCallPro: If DHCP issues persist after router restart and configuration, contact your ISP or a network technician for router troubleshooting.
videoSearchQuery: Ring doorbell Error 4 DHCP IP address fix

faq.q: What is DHCP?
faq.a: DHCP (Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol) is the system that automatically assigns IP addresses to devices on your network. Your router typically handles this.

faq.q: How do I find the Ring device's MAC address?
faq.a: In the Ring app, go to the device settings and look for Device Health or Network Information. The MAC address is listed there.

title: No Internet Connection
shortDescription: The Ring device is connected to WiFi but cannot reach Ring's cloud servers.
fullDescription:
Error 5 indicates that the Ring device has connected to your WiFi network and obtained an IP address, but cannot reach Ring's servers on the internet. This is typically a DNS or gateway issue on the router, an ISP outage, or a firewall blocking the Ring device's traffic.

Ring devices require a persistent internet connection to function. They communicate with Ring's cloud servers for live view, motion alerts, video recording, and device management.

Checking your internet connection with another device and restarting your router are the first steps to resolving this error.

causes.cause: Internet service outage from ISP
Router DNS settings misconfigured
Firewall or parental controls blocking Ring traffic
Ring server outage

symptoms: Error 5 in Ring app during setup or use
Ring device connected to WiFi but shows offline
Live view fails to load
Motion notifications stop arriving

fixSteps.title: Check internet connection
fixSteps.instruction: Use another device (phone or computer) to verify your internet is working. Try loading a website or running a speed test.

fixSteps.title: Restart router and modem
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug both your modem and router for 30 seconds. Plug in the modem first, wait 1 minute, then plug in the router. Wait 2-3 minutes for full restart.

fixSteps.title: Check firewall and DNS settings
fixSteps.instruction: In your router settings, ensure no firewall rules are blocking Ring traffic. Set DNS to automatic or use Google DNS (8.8.8.8) or Cloudflare DNS (1.1.1.1).

fixSteps.title: Check Ring server status
fixSteps.instruction: Visit status.ring.com or check Ring's social media accounts for any reported outages. If Ring servers are down, wait for the issue to be resolved.

whenToCallPro: Contact your ISP if your internet is down. Contact Ring support if the error persists with a working internet connection.
videoSearchQuery: Ring doorbell Error 5 no internet fix

faq.q: Can Ring work without internet?
faq.a: No. Ring devices require a constant internet connection for all features including live view, recording, and notifications. Without internet, the device cannot function.

faq.q: How much bandwidth does Ring need?
faq.a: Ring recommends at least 2 Mbps upload speed for standard quality video and 4+ Mbps for HD video. Poor upload speed can cause connectivity issues.

title: Bluetooth Connection Failed During Setup
shortDescription: The Ring app cannot establish a Bluetooth connection with the device during setup.
fullDescription:
Error 10 occurs during the initial setup process when the Ring app cannot establish a Bluetooth connection with the Ring device. Ring uses Bluetooth for the initial pairing and WiFi configuration process before the device switches to WiFi for normal operation.

This error is commonly caused by Bluetooth being disabled on the phone, the Ring device being too far from the phone, interference from other Bluetooth devices, or the phone's Bluetooth stack being in an inconsistent state.

Ensuring Bluetooth is enabled, being within close range, and restarting the phone typically resolves this issue.

causes.cause: Bluetooth disabled on the smartphone
Phone too far from the Ring device
Bluetooth interference from other devices
Phone Bluetooth stack needs reset
Ring app needs location permission for Bluetooth

symptoms: Error 10 in Ring app during initial setup
App says 'Searching for device' indefinitely
Setup fails at the Bluetooth pairing step
Ring device LED spins but app cannot find it

fixSteps.title: Enable Bluetooth on your phone
fixSteps.instruction: Open your phone's Settings, ensure Bluetooth is turned on. On both iOS and Android, Bluetooth must be active for Ring setup.

fixSteps.title: Grant location permission to Ring app
fixSteps.instruction: On Android, Bluetooth scanning requires location permission. Go to Settings > Apps > Ring > Permissions and enable Location. On iOS, enable Bluetooth permission for Ring.

fixSteps.title: Move close to the Ring device
fixSteps.instruction: Stand within 3-5 feet of the Ring device during setup. Bluetooth range is limited and walls or metal objects reduce it further.

fixSteps.title: Restart phone and Ring device
fixSteps.instruction: Restart your smartphone. Press and hold the setup button on the Ring device for 20 seconds to reboot it. Wait for the device LED to indicate setup mode, then retry.

fixSteps.title: Clear Ring app cache
fixSteps.instruction: On Android: Settings > Apps > Ring > Storage > Clear Cache. On iOS: Delete and reinstall the Ring app. Then retry setup.

whenToCallPro: If the Bluetooth pairing consistently fails after trying all steps, contact Ring support. The device may have a hardware Bluetooth issue requiring replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Ring doorbell Error 10 Bluetooth setup fix

faq.q: Why does Ring need Bluetooth?
faq.a: Ring uses Bluetooth only during initial setup to transfer your WiFi credentials to the device. After setup, Ring communicates entirely over WiFi.

faq.q: Why does Ring need location permission?
faq.a: On Android, Bluetooth Low Energy scanning requires location permission due to how the operating system handles Bluetooth. This is an Android requirement, not a Ring data collection feature.

title: Setup Interrupted — Device Timed Out
shortDescription: The Ring device setup process was interrupted or timed out before completion.
fullDescription:
Error 19 occurs when the Ring setup process is interrupted or times out. This can happen when the app loses connection to the device, when the phone switches networks during setup, or when the setup takes too long and the Ring device exits setup mode.

Ring devices have a limited window of time in setup mode (typically 10-15 minutes). If setup is not completed within this window, the device exits setup mode and Error 19 is displayed.

Restarting the setup process and having all credentials ready before beginning usually prevents this error.

causes.cause: Setup process took too long and timed out
Phone switched from Ring's setup WiFi back to home WiFi
Ring app crashed or was backgrounded during setup
Low battery on Ring device during setup

symptoms: Error 19 in Ring app after setup attempt
Setup progress bar freezes or disappears
'Setup timed out' message
Ring device LED stops spinning and turns off

fixSteps.title: Put the device back in setup mode
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the orange setup button on the Ring device for 20 seconds until the LED flashes. This re-enters setup mode.

fixSteps.title: Have credentials ready before starting
fixSteps.instruction: Before starting setup, have your WiFi network name and password ready. Keep the Ring app in the foreground and do not switch apps.

fixSteps.title: Stay close to the device
fixSteps.instruction: Remain within 5 feet of the Ring device throughout the entire setup process. Do not walk away during setup.

fixSteps.title: Charge the device before setup
fixSteps.instruction: For battery-powered Ring devices, charge to at least 50% before starting setup. Low battery can cause the device to exit setup mode prematurely.

whenToCallPro: If the device repeatedly fails setup even when following all steps, contact Ring support for further troubleshooting or a possible replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Ring doorbell Error 19 setup timeout fix

faq.q: How long does Ring stay in setup mode?
faq.a: Ring devices typically stay in setup mode for 10-15 minutes after pressing the setup button. If setup is not completed, you need to press the button again.

faq.q: Can I set up Ring without the app?
faq.a: No. Ring devices require the Ring app on a smartphone for initial setup. There is no web-based or computer setup option.

title: Ring Server Connection Failed
shortDescription: The Ring device cannot connect to Ring's cloud servers for activation.
fullDescription:
Error 21 indicates that the Ring device connected to WiFi and the internet but could not reach Ring's activation servers. This prevents the device from completing setup or registering with your Ring account.

This error can be caused by a temporary Ring server outage, DNS resolution failure, or network-level blocking of Ring's server endpoints. Corporate or school networks with strict firewalls commonly cause this error.

Checking Ring's server status and ensuring no firewall is blocking Ring traffic are the primary troubleshooting steps.

causes.cause: Ring server temporarily unavailable
Firewall or network restriction blocking Ring servers
DNS resolution failure for Ring endpoints
ISP blocking Ring traffic

symptoms: Error 21 displayed during setup activation step
'Cannot connect to Ring servers' message
Setup completes WiFi connection but fails at activation
Device shows connected but not activated in app

fixSteps.title: Check Ring server status
fixSteps.instruction: Visit status.ring.com or check DownDetector for Ring to see if there is a known outage. If so, wait and retry later.

fixSteps.title: Restart router and retry
fixSteps.instruction: Restart your router to clear any DNS cache issues. Wait 3 minutes, then retry the Ring setup.

fixSteps.title: Change DNS servers
fixSteps.instruction: In your router settings, change DNS servers to Google (8.8.8.8, 8.8.4.4) or Cloudflare (1.1.1.1, 1.0.0.1). This ensures Ring's domain names resolve correctly.

fixSteps.title: Try a different network
fixSteps.instruction: If on a restricted network (corporate, school, hotel), try setting up the Ring device on a different network such as a mobile hotspot from your phone.

whenToCallPro: If Error 21 persists with a working internet connection and no Ring outage, contact Ring support for server-side troubleshooting.
videoSearchQuery: Ring doorbell Error 21 server connection fix

faq.q: How do I check if Ring servers are down?
faq.a: Visit status.ring.com for official status updates, or check DownDetector.com for user-reported Ring outages.

faq.q: Can I use Ring on a corporate network?
faq.a: Corporate networks often block IoT device traffic. You may need IT department assistance to whitelist Ring's server endpoints, or use a separate network.

title: Hardware Setup Failure
shortDescription: The Ring device encountered a hardware error during the setup process.
fullDescription:
Error 40 is a hardware-related setup error indicating that the Ring device itself has a problem preventing setup from completing. This can be caused by a manufacturing defect, damage during shipping, or a corrupted firmware on the device.

Unlike network errors (Error 2-5), Error 40 typically points to an issue with the Ring device hardware or firmware rather than the network or setup process. The device may need a factory reset or, in some cases, replacement.

A factory reset resolves many Error 40 instances, especially if they were caused by a corrupted firmware state.

causes.cause: Corrupted device firmware
Manufacturing defect
Physical damage to internal components
Battery not properly seated (battery models)

symptoms: Error 40 displayed in Ring app during setup
Device LED behaves abnormally during setup
Setup fails at the same point every time
Device does not respond to setup button

fixSteps.title: Factory reset the Ring device
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the setup button on the Ring device for 20-25 seconds until the front LED flashes rapidly. Release and wait 5 minutes for the device to reboot and enter setup mode.

fixSteps.title: Check the battery (battery models)
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and reinsert the battery pack, ensuring it clicks securely into place. Charge the battery to at least 50% before attempting setup.

fixSteps.title: Update the Ring app
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure you have the latest version of the Ring app from the App Store or Google Play Store. Outdated apps may have setup compatibility issues.

fixSteps.title: Contact Ring support for replacement
fixSteps.instruction: If Error 40 persists after a factory reset, contact Ring support. The device may have a hardware defect requiring replacement under warranty.

whenToCallPro: If a factory reset does not resolve Error 40, the device likely has a hardware defect and should be replaced through Ring's warranty process.
videoSearchQuery: Ring doorbell Error 40 hardware setup fix

faq.q: Does Error 40 mean my Ring is broken?
faq.a: Not always. A factory reset resolves many Error 40 cases. If the reset does not help, the device may have a hardware issue requiring replacement.

faq.q: Will Ring replace a defective device?
faq.a: Ring offers a one-year warranty on all devices. If the device is defective and within warranty, Ring will typically provide a replacement. Contact Ring support to initiate the process.

title: WiFi Connection Lost or Unstable
shortDescription: The Ring device has lost its WiFi connection or is experiencing an unstable signal.
fullDescription:
The WiFi Error on Ring devices indicates that the device has lost its connection to the WiFi network or the signal is too weak for reliable operation. Ring devices need a consistent WiFi signal with adequate bandwidth for video streaming and cloud communication.

This error frequently appears when the Ring device is installed far from the router, when there is interference from other electronic devices, or when the WiFi network has changed (new router, changed password, different channel).

Ring recommends a minimum RSSI (Received Signal Strength Indicator) of -65 dBm for reliable operation. The Ring app shows the WiFi signal strength in Device Health.

causes.cause: Ring device too far from the WiFi router
WiFi interference from other devices or neighboring networks
Router rebooted or WiFi password changed
Router bandwidth congested by other devices
Ring device WiFi antenna degraded

symptoms: Device shows offline in Ring app
Live View fails or takes very long to load
Motion notifications delayed or missing
Video recordings are choppy or unavailable
Device Health shows poor WiFi signal (RSSI worse than -70)

fixSteps.title: Check WiFi signal strength
fixSteps.instruction: In the Ring app, go to your device > Device Health > Network. Check the RSSI value. Good is -40 to -50, acceptable is -50 to -65, poor is worse than -65.

fixSteps.title: Move the router or add a WiFi extender
fixSteps.instruction: If signal is poor, move the router closer to the Ring device, or install a WiFi extender or mesh WiFi node between the router and the Ring device. Ring sells a Chime Pro with built-in WiFi extender.

fixSteps.title: Reconnect to WiFi
fixSteps.instruction: In the Ring app, go to Device Settings > WiFi Settings > Change WiFi Network. Follow the prompts to reconnect to your network with the current password.

fixSteps.title: Change the router WiFi channel
fixSteps.instruction: In your router settings, switch the 2.4 GHz channel to 1, 6, or 11 — whichever has the least interference. Use a WiFi analyzer app to find the least congested channel.

whenToCallPro: If WiFi issues persist despite good signal strength and proper configuration, contact Ring support. The device's WiFi module may be failing.
videoSearchQuery: Ring doorbell WiFi disconnected signal fix

faq.q: What is a good RSSI for Ring?
faq.a: RSSI of -40 to -50 is excellent, -50 to -65 is good, and anything worse than -65 may cause reliability issues. Check in Ring app > Device Health.

faq.q: Should I get the Ring Chime Pro?
faq.a: If your Ring device has poor WiFi signal, the Ring Chime Pro acts as both a chime and a WiFi extender, which can significantly improve connectivity for Ring devices.

title: Live View Stream Cannot Be Established
shortDescription: The Ring app cannot establish a live video stream from the device.
fullDescription:
Live View Error occurs when the Ring app attempts to stream live video from the device but cannot establish the connection. This can be caused by insufficient WiFi bandwidth, poor signal strength, Ring server issues, or the device being in a sleep state and failing to wake up quickly enough.

Ring devices require at least 2 Mbps upload speed for standard quality live view. HD and 1080p streams require more bandwidth. Battery-powered Ring devices may take longer to initiate live view as they need to wake from a low-power state.

This is one of the most commonly reported Ring issues and usually relates to network performance rather than a device defect.

causes.cause: Insufficient WiFi bandwidth or upload speed
Poor WiFi signal at device location
Ring server congestion or temporary outage
Battery-powered device slow to wake up
VPN or ad-blocker on phone interfering

symptoms: 'Live View Error' message in Ring app
Black screen when tapping Live View
Spinning loading indicator that never resolves
'Activating Device' message that times out
Video loads briefly then disconnects

fixSteps.title: Check WiFi signal and speed
fixSteps.instruction: In Ring app, go to Device Health to check RSSI. Run a speed test on your phone while near the Ring device. Ensure at least 2 Mbps upload.

fixSteps.title: Restart the Ring device
fixSteps.instruction: For hardwired devices, toggle the breaker or disconnect power for 30 seconds. For battery devices, remove and reinsert the battery. Wait 2 minutes before trying Live View.

fixSteps.title: Force close and reopen the Ring app
fixSteps.instruction: Force close the Ring app completely (swipe up on iOS, or clear from recent apps on Android). Reopen it and try Live View again.

fixSteps.title: Disable VPN and ad-blockers
fixSteps.instruction: If you use a VPN or DNS-based ad-blocker on your phone, temporarily disable it and retry Live View. These can interfere with the peer-to-peer video stream.

fixSteps.title: Improve WiFi coverage
fixSteps.instruction: Add a WiFi extender or mesh node between your router and the Ring device. The Ring Chime Pro is specifically designed for this purpose.

whenToCallPro: If Live View consistently fails with strong WiFi and good internet speed, contact Ring support for advanced troubleshooting. The device may need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Ring doorbell Live View not working fix

faq.q: Why is Ring Live View so slow?
faq.a: Live View performance depends heavily on your WiFi upload speed and signal strength at the Ring device location. Battery-powered devices also need time to wake from sleep mode, adding delay.

faq.q: Does Live View use a lot of data?
faq.a: Yes, live video streaming uses significant data. A 5-minute Live View session uses approximately 50-100 MB depending on video quality settings.
